title: Competitor EDA: Unit Test Coverage for Derivation Logic
purpose: Add unit tests for the three non-trivial derivation paths in buildCompetitorAnalyticsPayload: postsPerActiveDay (active-day deduplication), avgViewsPerPost (notice exclusion regardless of excludeNotices param), and focusAuthor heatmap filter (client-side author cell tagging). Also covers parseAnalyticsQuery validation paths.
design_intent: Pure unit tests with no server, no file I/O, no env vars. Tests run via Node.js built-in test runner (tsx --test). Test data constructed inline as minimal ActivityLog arrays. No mocking framework required.
change_impact: Adds tests/unit/competitorAnalytics.test.ts. Adds test:unit script to package.json. No changes to library or app code.

test_targets.description: Author with 3 posts on 2 distinct calendar days (via postDateParsedMs) should yield postsPerActiveDay=1.5 and activeDays=2.
test_targets.why: The activeDays deduplication uses postDateParsedMs ?? firstSeenAtMs to derive the calendar day. If the ISO date field parses, the date — not the snapshot time — determines the active day. A naive implementation using firstSeenAtMs only would overcount active days when multiple posts share a board date.

test_targets.description: Author with 5 notice posts (0 views) and 10 regular posts (50 views each): avgViewsPerPost=50, avgViewsPostCount=10, postsInRange=15. excludeNotices=false so all 15 posts appear in events.
test_targets.why: G-002 fix: avgViewsPerPost must use nonNotice denominator regardless of excludeNotices. Pre-fix formula (totalViews/postsInRange) would yield 33, not 50.

test_targets.description: Author with only regular posts: avgViewsPostCount === postsInRange and avgViewsPerPost computed correctly. Confirms the baseline case is not broken by the fix.
test_targets.why: Regression guard: when there are no notice posts, avgViewsPostCount should equal postsInRange so the UI footnote is never shown.

test_targets.description: With two authors posting, focusAuthor=authorA → all heatmap cells carry author===authorA. With focusAuthor=null → cells from both authors present.
test_targets.why: G-001 fix: server tags each heatmap cell with its author. The cell.author field is what the client uses to filter in memory. If author tags are absent or wrong, the client-side filter silently produces an empty heatmap.

test_targets.description: from > to returns {error}. Unparseable date string returns {error}. Valid params return a CompetitorAnalyticsQuery with correct types.
test_targets.why: Ensures the query parser is a complete gate — invalid input never reaches buildCompetitorAnalyticsPayload.

test_targets.description: No params → fromMs is within 1 second of (now - 3 days), bucket defaults to 'day', excludeNotices defaults to false.
test_targets.why: The 3-day default was changed from 30 in the v2 polish session. This test pins the regression surface.

test_helpers.makeLog: Helper that constructs a minimal valid ActivityLog with a timestamp and post list.
test_helpers.makeQuery: Helper that builds a CompetitorAnalyticsQuery with sensible defaults, overridable per test.
test_helpers.eventSequence: Pattern: pass an array of [{ts, posts}] snapshots; each consecutive pair generates new-post events for posts that appear in curr but not prev.

acceptance_criteria: All 6 test targets have at least one passing test case
tsc --noEmit still passes (no type errors introduced)
No changes to lib/competitorAnalytics.ts or src/AnalyticsPage.tsx
out_of_scope: Frontend component tests (React/DOM — no test renderer in this project)
Bot signal heuristic tests (computeBotSignalsForAuthor — numerically complex, low regression risk)
